title: Use Chinese pause punctuation in Chinese body text

detect.signal: Web-facing Chinese prose uses ASCII comma, period, question mark, exclamation mark, semicolon, or colon as sentence punctuation where Chinese pause punctuation would be expected.
ignore_when: The punctuation is part of a URL, email address, file path, code token, command, decimal number, version number, product identifier, or protocol syntax.
The content is a formula-heavy technical document, Western-language grammar example, or other Project Override where Western punctuation is intentionally used for consistency.
The punctuation appears inside a quoted Western sentence or untranslated source title.
The text is not rendered to end users, such as source comments, internal documentation, debug logs, or synthetic fixtures.
The project has a documented editorial style that intentionally uses Western punctuation for this surface.

rationale: CLReq treats Chinese pause marks such as `。`, `，`, and `、` as core Chinese punctuation, and says Chinese-style punctuation should be used in principle when the main text is Chinese. Agents should review ASCII sentence punctuation in Chinese prose, while respecting technical and editorial exceptions.

clreq.title: Requirements for Chinese Text Layout (CLReq)
clreq.section: Phrase & section boundaries: Categories and usage of punctuation marks

clreq.title: Requirements for Chinese Text Layout (CLReq)
clreq.section: Characters & encoding: Composition of Chinese and Western Mixed Texts

examples.context: Chinese UI prose with ASCII comma and period.
examples.bad: 今天发布新功能, 请升级到最新版本.
examples.good: 今天发布新功能，请升级到最新版本。

examples.context: Chinese i18n message with a technical version number that should keep its decimal point.

agent_guidance.comment_template:
[normal] clreq.punctuation.chinese-pause-marks: Chinese prose is using ASCII sentence punctuation.

影响： CLReq describes Chinese pause punctuation as part of Chinese text layout, and ASCII punctuation can make Chinese UI copy look inconsistent unless a technical or editorial exception applies.
Suggested change: Replace sentence punctuation in the Chinese prose with Chinese punctuation such as `，` and `。`, while preserving punctuation inside URLs, versions, code, formulas, and quoted Western text.
Source: CLReq, Categories and usage of punctuation marks; CLReq, Composition of Chinese and Western Mixed Texts.
Confidence: high
agent_guidance.autofix_notes: Do not blindly replace all ASCII punctuation. If the user asks for edits, only change punctuation that is clearly sentence punctuation in Web-facing Chinese prose and leave technical tokens unchanged.
agent_guidance.decision_notes: If the surface is formula-heavy, Western-language teaching material, or has an explicit editorial override, downgrade to advisory or suppress the suggestion according to project policy.
